notes.anchor: I will not say his good offices, but his deserts
notes.note: Cicero draws a Roman distinction between officia (routine reciprocal favours between friends) and merita (genuine services creating a debt of gratitude); he insists Lamia's claims on him are of the stronger, obligating kind.

notes.anchor: but the canvassing seems to be stirred up in such a way
notes.note: The Latin ambitus covers vigorous electoral canvassing shading into outright bribery; Cicero fears Lamia's rivals are mounting so aggressive a campaign that he must take charge of Lamia's candidacy himself.
